feedback.text: Digital Fairness Act is something I can really get behind. I got introduced to it recently and I am really happy that positive things are happening on a larger scale in the digital field. However I feel like the issue of game developers not leaving their games in a playable state after ending their live support (see: Stop Killing Games EU Initiative) could be included to the Digital Fairness Act. See, I bought a game called "The Crew" from Ubisoft for 60 a few years back. It was not said anywhere that the game could not be played after 31.3.2024, when the servers were shutdown. I even tried contacting Ubisoft for a refund, but they were not going to refund me anything. This is a personal experience from a widespread issue in the gaming industry and I think that Digital Fairness Act is could be a great tool to try to fix it.
